content.seo_title: Master Debugging: Client Workflow for AI App Developers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn an expert-level client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster and build professional AI applications.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about guessing; it's about systematic isolation. By implementing a standardized client workflow using Antigravity for environment mapping and Claude Code for iterative analysis, you transform opaque errors into actionable logs. Stop fighting your code and start engineering predictable, high-performance AI solutions with professional-grade diagnostic clarity.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal overflowing with stack traces, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat monitor for a project that feels like it's flatlining. You’ve changed one variable, and now the entire architecture is screaming. This is the 'Junior Developer Wall,' and it’s the exact moment most aspiring builders quit. But here is the secret: top-tier engineers don't debug harder; they debug smarter using rigid, repeatable workflows.

Today, we’re replacing that anxiety with a high-velocity client workflow. We are moving beyond trial-and-error patching and into a domain of surgical precision. By leveraging the combined power of Antigravity and Claude Code, you will stop being a victim of your codebase and start acting as its architect, systematically dismantling bugs before they ever reach production.

steps.title: Establish Environmental Baseline
steps.description: Before fixing anything, you must define the current state. Antigravity helps map your dependencies and environment conflicts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project environment and generate a dependency map identifying potential version conflicts or missing system requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'audit dependencies and environment configuration'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Vector
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse logs and pinpoint the exact module causing the failure, rather than hunting through the entire directory.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Examine the error stack trace provided. Isolate the specific function or API call triggering the crash and suggest three potential root causes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze stack trace in logs/error.log and identify the failing function'

steps.title: Simulate the Fix
steps.description: Never apply a fix directly to production code without a sandbox simulation. We use the workflow to verify logic before committing changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the identified root cause, generate a unit test that recreates the bug reliably.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a test case in tests/repro_bug.py to isolate the failure'

steps.title: Execute Surgical Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix using Claude Code’s interactive refactoring, ensuring the change adheres to existing project patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the proposed fix for the bug. Ensure the implementation maintains type safety and follows the established project architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply fix to identified function and verify against tests'

steps.title: Validate Integrity
steps.description: Final check to ensure the fix didn't introduce regressions elsewhere in the application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a regression analysis on the entire codebase after the recent changes. Report any side effects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run full suite of tests and perform linting check'

pro_tips: Always treat your error logs as 'system documentation'—if you can't read them, you aren't ready to fix the bug.
Use Antigravity to generate 'diff-maps' before committing any code; visual regression is your best friend.
When Claude Code suggests a fix, ask it to explain the 'why' behind the change to level up your own mental model.
Keep your terminal sessions short-lived; restarting the environment often clears ghosts that standard reloads miss.
If a bug persists for more than 30 minutes, step away; your best debugging tool is a fresh perspective.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully patched your first major bug using this workflow, apply it to a new feature branch to prevent regressions during active development.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the professional-grade debugging workflow that separates hobbyists from high-impact engineers. Stop guessing, start architecting, and watch your velocity skyrocket as you build increasingly complex, production-ready AI applications. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Claude Code to parse logs and pinpoint the exact module causing the failure, rather than hunting through the entire directory.
Never apply a fix directly to production code without a sandbox simulation. We use the workflow to verify logic before committing changes.
Apply the fix using Claude Code’s interactive refactoring, ensuring the change adheres to existing project patterns.
Final check to ensure the fix didn't introduce regressions elsewhere in the application.
